MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar May-02-2006 Case Settled - Set For Crc 525 Hearing On Aug-01-06 At 10:00 A.M. In Dept 212
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly for trial by jury.

Appearances:
Nancy Conway, Esquire on behalf of the Plaintiff, Trisha Norkett (present)
Sara B. Allman, Esquire on behalf of the Defendants Riyad R. Salma, Karim A. Salma,
Roberta Salma, Laith Salma, Lindsey Y. Salma Bruell nee Lindsdey Y. Salma
and Triterra Realty Group (defendants not present)

This matter settled after being sent to a Judge Pro Tem. The terms and conditions of the settlement were put on the record. The terms are confidential.

Judge Pro Tem Richard Warren; Clerks: Vera Mu/LEspy; Reporter: Helen Sorep-Matley 225 (206)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Pltf To Provice Further Responses To Discovery And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Apr-13-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Paul A. Conroy for defendant and moving party Riyad Salma and Karim Salma
Nancy M. Conway for plaintiff and opposing party Trish Norkett
HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted:
1) Defendant Karim Salma's motion to compel further responses to request for production set #1 is Granted in part. Plaintiff is ordered to produce responsive documents from January 1, 1998 to the present by April 21, 2006. If plaintiff has previously produced all responsive documents, that representation shall be verified.

2) Defendant Riyad Salma's motion to compel further responses to special interrogatories set #2 is Granted. No opposition has been filed. The objections are overruled and stricken. Plaintiff is ordered to serve complete verified responses by April 21, 2006. The request for monetary sanctions is denied.
Compliance date for interrogatories is April 25, 2006. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-13-2006 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Oct-24-2005 Set For Trial On May-01-06 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206; Settlement Conference On Apr-13-06 At 08:30 A.M. In Dept 318

Off Calendar Oct-24-2005 Off Calendar

MEDIATION ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-03-2006 Off Calendar

Motion To Compel Pltf To Providefurther Responses To Discovery And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jan-06-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

NO APPEARANCES. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Defendant's motion to compel further responses to form interrogatories, special interrogatories set #1 and #2, and request for production of documents: Ordered off calendar per CCP 1005 as it was not timely noticed. CCP Section 1005 requires that motions be served and filed 16 court days prior to the hearing, with five calendar days added if the motion was served by mail or two calendar days added if the motion was served by overnight mail. The motion was filed on December 12, 2005 and is untimely based on either proof of service filed with the Court. In addition, a hearsay proof of personal service is not competent evidence that the motion was served by express mail. COMM: BRUCE E. CHAN
